content.full_text:
October 21, 1991                                    VIA FACSIMILE and MAIL


State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA  95812

Attention: Scott Hallabrin, Acting General Counsel

RE: Our Clients: Site Constructors, Inc.
    Application for Use Permit

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

     This letter will serve as a request for a formal opinion by
the Fair Political Practices Commission as to the potential
conflict of interest by supervisor Ed McLaughlin in voting on the
application for Use Permit by my client, Site Constructors, Inc. I
have been authorized by Supervisor Ed McLaughlin, through Neil
McCabe, Deputy County Counsel for the County of Butte, to seek this
opinion to attempt to resolve this issue.

                         FACTUAL SUMMARY

     Site Constructors, Inc. has made application before the Butte
County Board of Supervisors for a Use Permit to enlarge their use
of commercial property located in Butte County, California.
Supervisor Ed McLaughlin proposes to vote on the application for
the Use Permit.  Located directly across the street from the
property owned by Site Constructors, Inc. (approximately 60 feet
away) is a property owned by Hester Patrick.  This particular parcel
of property has been operated on a Lease Option for the previous
fifteen years by the Meline family and the Rabo family, in the
nature of a farming business.  Jack Meline is the manager of the
parcel of property, upon which an orchard crop is maintained.  As
is the nature of Supervisor McLaughlin's general business, he has
performed backhoe and other work on the subject real property
leased by Meline and Rabo during past years.  Specifically
Supervisor McLaughlin performed backhoe work last year on the
subject real property.  Supervisor McLaughlin indicates that he
obtains anywhere between $8,000.00 and $25,000.00 per year income

_________________________________________________________________
Refuge Business Plaza
1521 Butte House Road, Suite C        Yuba City, CA 95993        (916) 674-5996        Telefax (916) 674-7886


If there is specific text you need transcribed from this document, please provide a clearer or higher resolution image, or let me know if you need me to note that the content is [illegible].

Fair Political Practices Commission
RE: Site Constructors, Inc.
October 21, 1991
Page 2


for backhoe services performed as an independant contractor on the
subject real property for Meline and Rabo.

According to the enclosed exercise of option, the Meline and
Rabo families have exercised their option to purchase the subject
real property, effective December 2, 1991. The granting, or
denial, of Use Permit on the proposed parcel of property owned
by Site Constructors, Inc. will have no monetary effect on the
parcel of property currently owned by Hester Patrick and leased by
Meline and Rabo. Further, the grant or denial of the Use Permit
will have no financial effect on the assets or business of the
Meline and Rabo families.

QUESTIONS PRESENTED

1. Will a vote by Supervisor Ed McLaughlin on the application
for a Use Permit on the property of Site Constructors, Inc.
constitute a conflict of interest due to his continuing as an
independant contractor on adjacent lands leased by Meline and Rabo,
before the option date of December 2, 1991?

2. Will the opinion as to Question No. 1 above change after
December 2, 1991 when Meline and Rabo become owners of the subject
real property?

ANALYSIS AS TO QUESTION NO. 1

It must first be determined whether Supervisor McLaughlin's
economic interest is directly involved and whether the effect of
that decision is material pursuant to Title 2, California Code of
Regulations §18702.1 (all further citations to the California Code
of Regulations shall be to Title 2.)(§18702(a)). Since Supervisor
McLaughlin has no interest whatsoever in Site Constructors, Inc.,
his economic interest is not directly involved. Therefore, it must
be determined whether the effect of Supervisor McLaughlin's
decision is material under the appropriate regulations of §§18702.2
through 18702.6 (§18702(a))

While Meline and Rabo are a direct source of income to
Supervisor McLaughlin, their interests are not directly involved in
a decision before his agency, therefore Supervisor McLaughlin's
decision does not involve his economic interests directly involved
in the decision. (§18702.1(a)(1).)

The effect of the decision will not increase or decrease the
gross revenues for a fiscal year of Meline or Rabo by $10,000.00 or


Fair Political Practices Commission
RE: Site Constructors, Inc.
October 21, 1991
Page 3


more; will not result in their incurring or avoiding additional
expenses or eliminating existing expenses in a fiscal year in an
amount of $2,500.00 or; will not result in the increase or decrease
in value of assets or liabilities of $10,000.00 or more.
(§18702.2(g).)

Section 18702.3 is currently inapplicable as Supervisor
McLaughlin has no direct, indirect or beneficial ownership interest
in the real property leased by Meline and Rabo.

The decision will not materially affect the leasehold interest
of Meline and Rabo in that:

(a) The decision will not change legally allowable use
of the lease property.

(b) It is not reasonably foreseeable that the lessee
will change the actual use of the property as a result
of the decision.

(c) It is not reasonably foreseeable that the decision
will result in a change in the actual property within 300
feet of the leased property, and the changed use will not
significantly enhance or significantly decrease the use
or enjoyment of the leased property.

(d) The decision will not increase or decrease the
amount of rent for the leased property by $250.00 or 5%,
whichever is greater, during any 12 month period
following the decision.

(e) The decision will not result in a change in the
termination of the lease. (§18702.4.)

To the extent that Meline and Rabo can be considered
individuals, §18702.6 is inapplicable in that the decision will not
affect their income, investments or other tangible or intangible
assets by $1,000.00; or the decision will not affect the real
property interests, as previously stated under §18702.4.
(§18702.6.)

Based upon this analysis, it is concluded that Supervisor
McLaughlin has no conflict of interest and may properly vote on the
proposed Land Use Permit submitted by Site Constructors, Inc..


Fair Political Practices Commission
RE: Site Constructors, Inc.
October 21, 1991
Page 4


ANALYSIS OF QUESTION NO. 2

The only facts that have changed with regard to Question No.
2 is that Meline and Rabo will become owners of the subject real
property after December 2, 1991. All of the previous factors
remain the same, except the application of §18702.3, after December
2, 1991, needs to be considered. Since Supervisor Ed McLaughlin has
no direct, indirect or beneficial ownership interest in the real
property to be owned by Meline and Rabo, §18702.3 is indirectly
inapplicable, however, there exists the possibility that it could
be applicable by virtue of §18702.6(b) in that the decision will
effect the individuals real property interest in a manner
considered material under §18702.3. As indicated above, Meline and
Rabo are farming businesses, and should not be considered
individuals in this transaction. However, even if they are
considered individuals, there is no showing under §18702.3(a)(1)
that the decision will have any financial effect upon the real
property interest of Meline and Rabo.

Therefore, it is concluded that after December 2, 1991 that
Supervisor Ed McLaughlin will also not have a conflict of interest
and may properly vote on the proposed Use Permit by Site
Constructors, Inc.

It is hereby requested that your office confirm this analysis,
by formal writing, within the statutory period of time. We are
hoping to get this matter on for vote and consideration by
Supervisor McLaughlin as soon as possible. If possible during the
framework of current assignments, it would be appreciated if this
matter could be expedited as soon as possible.

Thank you for your kind attention to this matter.

Very truly yours,

MICHAEL R. BARRETT

MRB:kn

cc: Neil McCabe, Butte County District Attorney's Office
    Site Constructors, Inc.


NOTICE OF EXERCISE OF OPTION

TO: Hester Patrick, successor in interest of Garrison Patrick,
deceased, and Melissa B. Deter, Lessors and Optionors:

YOU, AND EACH OF YOU, WILL PLEASE TAKE NOTICE that Jack
Meline, Sharon Meline, Edward Richard Meline, Charlena Mary
Meline, Fred A. Rabo, Dorothy May Rabo, and Violet Arlene Meline
(who has succeeded to the interest of Stephen Meline III,
deceased), Lessees and Optionees under that certain written
Agreement for Lease and Option to Purchase Real Property and Sale
of Real Property (herein, "the agreement") do hereby exercise
their option to purchase Parcel 2 therein described pursuant to
and upon the terms and conditions of the Option to Purchase set
forth in the agreement (page 23, line 23, through page 27, line
18, both inclusive). Lessees and Optionees respectfully direct
your attention to the provisions of the agreement under the
caption "PAYMENT OF PRICE, ESCROW" (page 24, line 4, through page
26, line 8, both inclusive) concerning the establishment of the
escrow for the consummation of the sale of the real property.
(Inasmuch as the sale date December 1, 1991, specified in the
agreement falls upon a Sunday, the sale date under California law
will be Monday, December 2, 1991.)

Dated: July 22, 1991.

Jack Meline                    Sharon Meline

Edward Richard Meline         Charlena Mary Meline

Fred A. Rabo                   Dorothy May Rabo

                               Violet Arlene Meline

ROBERT B. KUTZ
NOTARY PUBLIC
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
TEL (619) 691-1451
FAX (619) 691-0630

October 3, 1991
10381 Midway
Chico, CA, 95928


To Whom it May Concern:
This will verify that Meline and Rabo Inc. have leased
421.35 acres from Hester Patrick on the Midway extending from
just north of what was known as Little Butte Creek to a point
just south of the Oroville Chico Road with Jack Meline as
manager of this acreage.

This property has been operated on a Lease Option to
buy, originally for ten years, however they asked for a five
year extension which expires December 1,1991. I have been
notified they will exercise the option on that date.

Hester G. Patrick

OFFICE OF COUNTY COUNSEL
COUNTY OF BUTTE
25 COUNTY CENTER DRIVE / OROVILLE, CALIFORNIA 95965-3380 / (916) 538-7621 / FAX (916) 538-7120

Oct 28 1991

NEIL H. McCABE
ASSISTANT COUNTY COUNSEL

SUSAN MINASIAN
COUNTY COUNSEL

DAVID M. McCLAIN
CHIEF DEPUTY COUNTY COUNSEL

GREGORY P. EINHORN
CHIEF DEPUTY COUNTY COUNSEL

October 25, 1991
VIA FAX and MAIL

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95812
Attn: Scott Hallabrin, Acting General Counsel

Re: Site Constructors, Inc.
Application for Use Permit

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

This office has received a copy of the letter to you from
Michael Barrette, Attorney at Law, dated October 21, 1991. We
would like to comment on the letter as follows.

The applicant for the use permit in question sought the permit
to expand a non-conforming use. The County Planning Commission
denied the permit, and the applicant appealed to the Board of
Supervisors.

The appeal came before the Board of Supervisors at a regular
meeting on September 17, 1991. Four Board members participated in
the hearing; the fifth, Supervisor Ed McLaughlin, absented himself
because of a perceived potential conflict of interest. (Please see
enclosed letter of October 8, 1991 from William H. Randolph, Chief
Administrative Officer (and also Clerk of the Board of
Supervisors). Said letter was addressed to Howard S. Horn, who is
the President of Site Constructors Inc.)

As indicated in said letter, at the conclusion of the Board of
Supervisors hearing a motion was made and seconded to uphold the
appeal; however, the motion did not pass since the vote on it was
a 2-2 tie. The result was that the Planning Commission denial of
the project became the final decision.

Site Constructors Inc. is seeking a reconsideration of the
matter by the Board of Supervisors with Supervisor McLaughlin

participating so that the tie can be broken. Title 2, California
Code of Regulations Section 18701(c)(1) precludes a disqualified
member from participating to break a tie. Therefore, in order for
Supervisor McLaughlin to participate it would be necessary to
conclude that his perceived potential conflict is not an actual
conflict and he is not precluded from participating.

We have advised Supervisor McLaughlin not to participate
unless a letter is received from you expressing the opinion that
there is no conflict. Supervisor McLaughlin has followed this
advice.

As Mr. Barrette has pointed out, the Use Permit Application
does not directly affect Supervisor McLaughlin. The question is
the affect, if any, on a source of his income. Would the expansion
of a non-conforming use by Site Constructors, Inc. have an affect
upon the source of Supervisor McLaughlin's income, which source
currently leases property directly across the street and has
exercised an option to purchase the leased property?

Thank you for your attention to these matters.


Very truly yours,

SUSAN MINASIAN
Butte County Counsel

By [illegible]
Neil H. McCabe
Assistant County Counsel

NHM/slt
(hallabrin)

cc: Michael R. Barrette, Attorney at Law

CHIEF ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICE
COUNTY OF BUTTE
25 COUNTY CENTER DRIVE
OROVILLE CALIFORNIA 95965-3280
Telephone: (916) 538-7631
Fax: (916) 538-7120

MEMBERS OF THE BOARD:

HASKEL A. McINTURF
JANE DOLAN
KEVIN CAMPBELL
ED McLAUGHLIN
LEN FULTON

WILLIAM H. RANDOLPH
CHIEF ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICER

October 8, 1991

Howard S. Horn
P.O. Box 6254
Chico, CA 95965

Re: Use Permit, AP 040-060-067

Dear Mr. Horn:

At the regular meeting of the Butte County Board of Supervisors on September 17, 1991,
your appeal of the Planning Commission's denial of a Use Permit to allow the expansion
of a nonconforming use to allow the pre-fabrication and storage of hydro-electric
equipment was heard. Four Board members participated in the hearing; the fifth
member absented himself because of a perceived potential conflict of interest.

At the conclusion of the hearing a motion was made and seconded to uphold the appeal;
however, the motion did not pass since the vote on it was a 2-2 tie. No further motion
was made. Therefore, the result of the Board proceedings was that no action, either to
uphold or deny the appeal, was taken by the Board, and the Commission's denial of the
project is the final decision on the project.

Should you have any questions regarding this matter, please contact the Planning
Department between 10:00 a.m. and 3:00 p.m.

Very truly yours,

William H. Randolph
Chief Administrative Officer

cc: Michael R. Barrett, Attorney
1521 Butte House Road, Suite C
Yuba City, CA 95993

sections.question:
1. Will a vote by Supervisor Ed Mclaughlin on the application for a Use Permit on the property of Site Constructors, Inc. constitute a conflict of interest due to his continuing as an independent contractor on adjacent lands leased by Meline and Rabo, before the option date of December 2, 1991?

2. Will the opinion as to Question No. 1 above change after December 2, 1991 when Meline and Rabo become owners of the subject real property?
sections.question_synthetic: Whether Supervisor Ed McLaughlin has a conflict of interest in voting on a use permit for Site Constructors, Inc., given his income from backhoe work performed on adjacent property leased (and later to be owned) by the Meline and Rabo families.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains incoming request letter dated October 21, 1991, supplemented by supporting documents (option exercise notice, letter from Hester Patrick, and County Counsel response dated October 25, 1991). The attorney-drafted request includes the requester's own legal analysis and cites specific regulations, which is atypical for an incoming request. The FPPC's actual response/opinion is not included in this document package.

2. Will the opinion as to Question No. 1 above change after December 2, 1991 when Meline and Rabo become owners of the subject real property?

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request letter from attorney Michael Barrette, authorized by Supervisor Ed McLaughlin, seeking an FPPC formal opinion on whether the Supervisor has a conflict of interest in voting on a use permit application. The letter includes detailed factual analysis and cites California Code of Regulations sections to argue no conflict exists before or after December 2, 1991.
